mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk assets reprice.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > 100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 70% of triggered instances, or individual series show no correlation with SPX.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST > 500k) and retail sales (RSAFS > 375B) indicate robust demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings benefit.
failure_condition: The dual-proxy signal fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or individual series show no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO > 94) reflects strong manufacturing activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as corporate margins improve.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no significant correlation with 5d SPX returns in 3-year rolling windows, or the signal generates negative returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth (GDPC1 < 1.5% annualized) signals economic stagnation, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 falls below 1.5% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 0.5pp change) during a recessionary regime signals deteriorating labor conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to produce negative 5d returns in > 70% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish FEDFUNDS trajectory combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 215) creates a 'stagflation' risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as central bank policy becomes ineffective.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or individual series show no predictive power.
